A systematic reliability study of Ga<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> MODFETs is carried out by deliberately probing vulnerable device region using particle strikes and by localizing the effects across the Gate – Drain access regions near the quantum well through angled ion strikes. Comparative analysis of the resulting drain current transients reveals an increased sensitivity to angled heavy ion strike towards the drain electrode, leading to a pronounced increase in peak transient current due to enhanced carrier collection at the drain electrode.

title: Next-Generation Biosensing: A Review on TFET-Based Sensors with Machine Learning (ML) Integration
authors: V.Karutharaja, N.B.Balamurugan, G.Vijaya Lakshmi, K.Kokulavani
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: In this review, summarizes the physics and device topologies of Tunnel FET-based biosensors, dielectric-modulation techniques, Two dimensional material platforms, manufacturing challenges and performance measures. Next, we discuss the growing integration of Artificial Intelligent (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) with biosensor data streams in order to improve the processing signal, false positive, false negative rejection and to achieve rapid classification and quantification of analytes. TFETs have emerged as a strong platform for ultra-low-power, highly sensitive label-free biosensing owing to their steep subthreshold swing and band-to-band tunneling characteristics. Finally, we assess outstanding challenges and future approaches for combining sophisticated machine learning pipelines with advancements in TFET technology to accelerate translational biosensing applications.

title: Hybrid Ensemble Learning for High-Precision DDoS Attack Detection in IoT Networks Using the INDDOS24 Dataset
authors: Usman Mustafa, Minahil Kamran, Muhammad Yousaf Rafique, Alina Akhtar, Ashas Mushtaq, Umer Majeed
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Machine Learning, and Intelligence (DataSciMI)
abstract: The rapid growth of the Internet of Things (IoT) resulted in several connected gadgets in the world never seen before, expected to continue to tens of billions by 2025. Although the development of IoT is efficient in smart homes, industrial automation, and healthcare, there is also an increase in the attack surface that cyber threats can attack. IoT devices are also being used more as a large-scale botnet in Distributed Denial-of-Service (DDoS) attacks. In recent times, there have been reports of multi-terabit DDoS attacks, novel IoT botnets (e.g., Mirai-based) with the ability to generate record-breaking volumes of traffic. Conventional signature-based systems (e.g., Snort, Cisco Firepower) have a hard time resisting such advanced attacks. In response to this, we will elaborate a hybrid machine-learning ensemble model based on new INDDOS24 data (an IoT DDoS benchmark). Our voting ensemble is soft (e.g., tuned classifiers, such as XGBoost, Random Forest, and a neural network) to obtain high detection precision. We also conduct a lot of data preprocessing and feature engineering on INDDOS24, use SHAP to have interpretability, and compare our results with the state of the art. Our model achieves more than 98% accuracy and F1-score, which is better than single-model baselines. Explanation analysis displays the important flow characteristics (e.g., the rate of packets, the duration of flows) upon which decisions are made. We comment on deployment efficiency (latency of the inference less than a second) and robustness, in comparison to the legacy IDS benchmarks, and describe their practical implication. Federated and transfer learning methods to real-time IoT security are also to be worked on in the future.

title:
Engineering of L-channel Tunnel Field Effect Transistor (L-Channel TFET) with high k material to reduce the I
                    <sub>off</sub>
                    current
authors: Sudha Rani, Ashish Raman
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: Proposed paper represent a novel approach modified L-channel tunnel field Effect Transistor (L-channel TFET) structure doped silicon source, which improve the subthreshold swing (SS), reduced I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">off</inf>, and the combination of dielectric material (Sio2) and the Molybdenum (Mo) create excellent effect of leakage current reduction. Using the low k-material at the drain to reduce the capacitance of gate–drain. It also improving in terms of downscaling Tunnel Field Effect Transistor.

title: A Comparative Performance Analysis of Fog-Assisted vs. Cloud-Only Architectures for a Real-Time Mood-Aware IoT System
authors: Prashanth Ragam, Harathi Chavala, Ashish Nerusu, Sai Ganesh Reddy Venna
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: Real-time internet of Things (IoT) applications based on continuous multimodal inputs, such as EEG and camera streams intended for mood detection, put demanding requirements in terms of latency, efficient bandwidth usage, and controlled energy consumption. Traditional Cloud-Only architectures are not suitable to meet these requirements due to the long network path and high transmission load involved. This paper presents a performance comparison between Fog-Assisted and Cloud-Only deployment models for a real-time mood-aware IoT system using iFogSim. Two scenarios are simulated by varying module placement: (1) Fog-Assisted, where computation happens on a local fog node, and (2) Cloud-Only, where processing will be offloaded completely onto the cloud. Key performance indicators-measured end-to-end latency, network usage, and total energy consumption under identical conditions-demonstrate that the Fog-Assisted model significantly reduces latency and WAN bandwidth usage and presents more efficient system-level energy consumption for continuous real-time workloads. These results highlight the suitability of fog architectures for intelligent, delay-sensitive IoT applications, and the presented work will lay the ground for future enhancements, including adaptive module placement, mobility support, and AI-driven edge inference. In comparison to the Cloud-Only model, the Fog-Assisted model demonstrated a 98% reduction in latency (4 ms vs 212 ms), an 80% reduction in network usage (10,000 units vs 50,000 units), and a 99.6% reduction in energy consumption (1.67 × 10 Ws vs 4.45 × 10 Ws). These results confirm fog computing’s suitability for intelligent and delay-sensitive applications and show its obvious superiority for real-time mood-aware IoT workloads. This work also lays the groundwork for future studies on AI-driven edge intelligence, mobility-aware processing, and adaptive module placement.

title: Analysis of Historical Speech Based on the Decision-Making Process of Political Leaders Using Natural Language Processing
authors: Varsha Mittal, Narendra Bisht, Kamola Oltiboyeva, Bakhromjan Djalalov, Madirimova Saodatjon Madirimovna, Nafisa Khudayberganova
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: Historical speeches of political leaders have served as among the most powerful parts in influencing the globe. Speeches of the textual form were imprinted into history. These kinds of conversations have a significant impact on the general population and their activities in the days ahead. Additionally, if allowed unsupervised, political persons or groups may generate severe issues. In numerous situations, there may be an indicator that the governing body has to adjust its regulations and simultaneously attend to the populace. Recognizing the sentimental analysis of a political statement is vital, as they can be preliminary signs or alerts for future worldwide emergencies, alliances, battles, and future wars. This study emphasized the prime ministers of the United States and India, categorizing their speeches according to their emotional content and context. In this study, optimism is a supplementary emotion, while happiness and upset are the main ones. Beyond categorizing the speeches depending on context and emotion, among the primary tasks of this study present a database of political speeches containing 2010 speeches annotated with context-based and emotion-based historical speeches. The speeches that are being focused on are quite lengthy. This study introduces EMPOLITICIAN-Context, an adaptive voting classification technique for context-based categorization, and EMPOLITICIAN-Emotion, an adaptive voting algorithm for emotion categorization of political speeches. The suggested EMPOLITICIAN-Context approach has obtained 73.2% accuracy in the area of context-based categorizing, and the EMPOLITICIAN-Emotion approach accomplished 53.1% accuracy in categorizing the emotion of the historical speeches of political leaders.

title: Efficient RTL to GDSII Layout Implementation and Verification of UART Protocol Using SystemVerilog and OpenLane
authors: Dhanavath Rajesh, Arvind Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: This paper outlines the entire process of designing an ASIC for a Universal Asynchronous Receiver and Transmitter (UART), from RTL to GDSII layout. Yosys begins by creating a gate-level netlist using Verilog's RTL design. Functional verification is thoroughly examined using SystemVerilog testbenches to ensure that it is accurate at the RTL level. The OpenLane toolchain, which includes the OpenROAD application, is used to automate the process of floorplanning, placement, routing, and timing closure for the physical design. The paper goes into more detail about the most important design problems that were faced and talks about possible ways to make things even better. We show simulation results with waveform outputs from different testing scenarios, as well as backend results that show the physical layout and Quality of Results metrics, to prove that the process works effectively, validating the capabilities of the open-source EDA toolchain for producing production-ready ASIC layouts. The importance of each phase evaluated in the ASIC development life cycle is discussed in the concluding sections, along with potential terminology for relating the measurement of design performance and potential solutions for future work that aims to achieve an order of magnitude higher operating frequencies.

title: Wavelet Neural Network-based Human Activity Recognition: A Gravity-Aware Hybrid Architecture
authors: Faizal Shanavas Puthiyaveettil, Ramanujam E, Ashutosh Sahoo, Ponnekanty Y Raj Kamal Tutu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: Human Activity Recognition (HAR) using wearable sensors is often restricted by noisy, high-dimensional data. Standard deep learning models frequently fail to capture fine-grained time-frequency details, leading to specific confusion between static postures such as "Sitting" and "Standing". This paper introduce a hybrid model featuring an Auto-Grouped Wavelet Layer that generates 511 features via a Power Set approach, and a dedicated Static Branch using Global Average Pooling. By isolating the gravity vector, the proposed architecture effectively resolves static ambiguity and ensures robust classification accuracy. This study evaluates the proposed architecture on the widely-used UCI Human Activity Recognition (UCI-HAR) dataset,where the proposed WNN-CNN and WNN-LSTM models achieved accuracies of 95% and 93% respectively, demonstrating significant improvements over baseline models.

title: Stretchable AgNP/AgNW Co-Deposited Conductive Fiber with PDMS Encapsulation for Patellar Tendon Reflex Strain Sensing
authors: Ying-Nien Chou, Po-Chun Huang, Cheng-You Chi, Kai-Sheng Hsieh, Wan-Jung Chang
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: This study presents a stretchable conductive fiber designed for dynamic patellar tendon reflex sensing, fabricated through AgNP/AgNW co-deposition on alkali-treated polyurethane (PU) fibers. The alkali pretreatment promotes silver ion absorption and enhances insitu nucleation of AgNPs, while subsequent AgNW deposition forms a continuous conductive network with improved mechanical compliance. To further enhance durability, a polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS) protective layer is applied, providing resistance against moisture, oxidation, and repeated bending during knee-tapping tests. When the patellar tendon is stimulated, the involuntary muscle contraction induces strain on the fiber, resulting in an immediate resistance change that can be used to quantify reflex onset, peak response, and response latency. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed conductive fiber exhibits stable electromechanical performance and reliable signal output during repetitive knee-tapping events, indicating its potential for wearable biomedical sensing applications and neuromuscular function evaluation.

title: Effectivity of Exposure Therapy Simulations in Video Games on Mitigating Social Anxiety
authors: Jasmine L. Gallaron, Marvi G. Fetiluna, Lieudwig A. Abella, Vicente F. Patalita
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: This study evaluates a 3D game-based simulation designed as a controlled aid for exposure therapy-aligned interventions for social anxiety. The platform integrates a Python-powered facial-emotion recognition (FER) module and IBM Watson-driven dialogue to monitor user mood and dynamically adjust session flow. Grounded in the APA Severity Measure for Social Anxiety Disorder, the research analyzed emotional patterns across varying social scenarios. Results demonstrate that the simulation effectively evokes scenarioappropriate responses: casual interactions yielded higher positive affect, while performance-based tasks-such as public speaking-successfully elicited neutral and negative emotions associated with evaluative stress. Notably, the 6% margin of positive over negative affect demonstrates the system's ability to maintain user engagement without exceeding manageable emotional thresholds. High usability ratings and successful emotional induction indicate the simulation is a viable tool for early-stage exposure-based support.

title: iNoodle: An Artificial Intelligence Seasoning-Packet Recognition System Applied to Packaged Dried Noodle Production Line
authors: Wan-Jung Chang, Ping-Ying She, Chia-Yu Wu, Jian-Ping Su
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: This paper proposes a seasoning-packet recognition system based on an AI-based deep learning model, called iNoodle. By designing an inspection chamber that integrates a camera module and an AI-edge platform with an inverted U-shaped base, iNoodle can be easily and cost-effectively deployed on a packaging line to detect abnormal quantities and types of seasoning packets. Experimental results demonstrate that iNoodle effectively and accurately identifies types and quantities of seasoning packets, enabling dried noodle manufacturers to eliminate the need for manual inspection and decrease inspection errors, thereby reducing production costs and improving product quality.

title: Character MBTI for Quantifying Narrative Atmosphere in Novel Recommendation
authors: Jikang Zhang, Yusong Zhou, Yuanyuan Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: Current novel recommendation systems often fail to capture the subtle narrative atmosphere that emerges from a story's characters and interactions. To address this limitation, we introduce a computational framework that quantifies narrative atmosphere using character Myers-Briggs Type Indicator (MBTI) profiling. The framework uses a character influence model that considers the quantity of dialogue and the semantic importance of speech, as measured by Term Frequency-Inverse Document Frequency (TF-IDF), to weigh character profiles derived from multi-model fusion. Our experiments demonstrate that conventional evaluation metrics are not well-suited to subjective tasks such as this one. Therefore, we propose an alternative evaluation approach based on Kendall's coefficient and paired win counts. It identifies the most effective models and reveals the limitations of simple averaging fusion strategies. Overall, this study offers a fresh perspective on deep text comprehension and provides a more reliable method for evaluating subjective ranking tasks.

title: On Taipei MRT Passenger Flow Prediction by Considering Multiple External Factors
authors: Yu-Wen Hung, Yu-Siou Siao, Kuei-Ping Shih
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: This paper aims to address the key challenge in metro passenger flow prediction: accurately capturing the influence of multiple external factors while overcoming the high computational cost of deep learning models. To balance the trade-off between accuracy and efficiency, this paper incorporates model compression techniques and proposes a Factor-Specialized Multi-Teacher Knowledge Distillation (FS-MTKD) framework, in which multiple expert teachers are specialized based on different external factors. Unlike traditional single-model approaches that attempt to learn all factor relationships simultaneously, this paper designs multiple expert teacher models, each dedicated to capturing the impact of a single external factor on passenger flow. Through Knowledge Distillation (KD), a lightweight student model is guided to absorb the heterogeneous knowledge distilled from all expert teachers and to achieve effective balancing among multiple influencing factors. The experimental results validate the effectiveness of the proposed method. After applying FS-MTKD, the student model achieves prediction accuracy comparable to that of more complex teacher models, while significantly reducing both model parameters and computational cost.

title: Sparse Matrix Accelerator Based on Dynamic Pruning
authors: Mei-Yun Chen, Yu-Cheng Fan, Ching-Min Lee
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: To accelerate model computation, this paper presents a dynamic token selector based on Vision Transformer, focusing primarily on pruning operations using a self-attention mechanism. To enable the model to adaptively retain key tokens and discard unimportant ones based on the importance of the input data, accordingly decreasing computational load and increasing efficiency, a module is added after the Softmax operation to dynamically identify and retain important tokens, directly using pre-calculated attention as the pruning basis, resulting in simpler hardware.

title: SUROY TA!: A Virtual City Simulator Exploring Historical Landmarks in Cebu City
authors: MA. Angelica Elimanco, Vicente Patalita, Temothy Homecillo
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: This project introduces an immersive virtual tourism platform that addresses the limitations of current services like Google Street View and YouTube 360 by offering real-time 3D exploration, spatial audio, and virtual tour narration. Focused on key landmarks in Cebu City, the platform enables users to freely navigate interactive environments enhanced with cultural and historical context. Designed for both VR and desktop computers, it prioritizes accessibility, educational value, and user engagement. By combining advanced graphics, virtual guides, and optimized asset use, the system provides a scalable and inclusive alternative to traditional tourism experiences.

title: Experimental Study of Timing Synchronization Techniques for Outdoor Mobile MIMO Transmission
authors: Haowen Bai, Hidekazu Murata, Satoshi Suyama, Huiling Jiang
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: In outdoor mobile environments, our multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) transmission experimental system experiences continuously varying timing offsets between transmitted and received samples. These variations arise from leading and delayed multipath components, as well as changes in radio propagation distance. As a result, timing synchronization fails, resulting in degradation of the bit error rate (BER). In this paper, we consider two synchronization algorithms that estimate and compensate for timing offsets between transmission and reception samples using correlation functions, and evaluate their performance experimentally. The experimental results show that both algorithms can compensate timing offsets in real time and reduce the BER, while one of them achieves a significantly greater improvement.

title: SpeakBright: A Mobile Application for Augmentative and Alternative Communication (AAC) for Nonverbal ASD Learners
authors: Jessa Donnalyn Reroma, Jovelyn C. Cuizon, Justin Antonio Bolivar, Ethan Ortiz
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: Children with Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) and speech impairments face significant communication challenges, impacting their development and quality of life. Existing solutions like the Picture Exchange Communication System (PECS) and Augmentative and Alternative Communication (AAC) apps have limitations, such as a lack of emotional expression support and progress tracking. This study introduces SpeakBright, a mobile application which combines traditional methods like PECS and Applied Behavior Analysis (ABA) and modern technologies to provide child friendly learning tool with adaptive recommendations, progress tracking, and completion estimation. The application adapts to a child's developmental stage, which allows children to expand their vocabulary, improve sentence formation, and support positive communication habits, enhancing both verbal and non-verbal abilities. Testing results indicate a high level of acceptability by care providers and ASD teachers of SpeakBright's data-driven functionalities in support of educational intervention.

title: A Novel Quality Model for Education Management Information Systems: Insights From the Openemis Case Study in Jordan
authors: Ahmad Althunibat, Addelrahim Emad Mehyar, Seif hashem Alazzam, Naif Alzriqat, Bassam M. Al-mahadeen
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Computer Theory and Applications (ICCTA)
abstract: Education Management Information Systems (EMIS) in modern educational administration, highlighting challenges such as rapid data processing, user accessibility, and advanced analysis tools that limit user satisfaction and data-driven decisionmaking. A mixed-methods approach was employed, involving a questionnaire completed by 519 EMIS users and semistructured interviews with 15 experts from organizations like UNESCO and the Jordanian Ministry of Education. Results indicated a need for improvements in real-time data processing and user accessibility, with statistical analysis showing these factors account for 74.7 % of the variance in user satisfaction. The study demonstrated strong reliability with a Cronbach's Alpha of 0.928, and user satisfaction ratings improved from 2.95 to 3.7 after implementing a proposed quality framework. Despite positive findings, experts emphasized resource constraints and the necessity for ongoing user education. The research aims to enhance EMIS, making it more user-friendly and responsive in educational management.

title: APEX-IDS: Advanced Performance Excellence IoT Detection System Using Deep Learning and Explainable AI
authors: Mostafa Mohamed, Saad M. Ibrahim, Amr A. Abohany, Muhammad Mahmoud
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Computer Theory and Applications (ICCTA)
abstract: The rapid proliferation of Internet of Things (IoT) devices has created unprecedented cybersecurity challenges, necessitating advanced intrusion detection systems capable of identifying sophisticated attack patterns in real-time. This paper presents APEX-IDS (Advanced Performance Excellence IoT Detection System), a novel deep learning framework that integrates convolutional neural networks (CNNs), long short-term memory (LSTM) networks, and explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) techniques for comprehensive IoT intrusion detection. Our multi-layered architecture combines spatial and temporal feature extraction with attention mechanisms to achieve superior detection accuracy while maintaining computational efficiency for edge deployment. Extensive evaluation on multiple benchmark datasets including CICIoT2023, UNSW-NB15, ToN_IoT, NSLKDD, and IoT-23 demonstrates exceptional performance with 99.23 % accuracy, 98.87 % precision, 99.15 % recall, 99.01 % F1score, and 0.996 AUC. The integration of SHAP and LIME explainability techniques provides transparent decision-making capabilities essential for operational deployment. Statistical significance testing confirms APEX-IDS superiority over existing approaches (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p}<0.001$</tex>), while maintaining practical deployment feasibility with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2. 3 m s}$</tex> detection time and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 5. 2 M B}$</tex> memory footprint.

title: Building a Framework Towards Intelligent Robotic Process Automation Capabilities
authors: Doha Rahma, Mohamed Aborizka, Menna Kamel
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Computer Theory and Applications (ICCTA)
abstract: To the best of our knowledge, this work introduces a framework that combines YOLOv7-based deep learning and real-time object detection with robotic process automation (RPA) to enhance decision-making. The goal is to enable bots to “see,” interpret visual elements, and choose actions dynamically instead of following static workflows. Traditional RPA systems often fail when dealing with unstructured data, unexpected layouts, or multilingual interfaces and usually depend on predefined rules, which leads to increased errors and human intervention. Our approach integrates deep learning for perception, transfer learning for adaptability across domains, and AI-driven decision modules to make automation more flexible and context-aware. Experimental results demonstrate notable improvements in accuracy, efficiency, and recovery from unpredictable scenarios. By linking real-time detection with autonomous decisions, the framework pushes RPA toward scalable, intelligent, and adaptive business process automation.

title: Open Agent Specification: Enabling Cross-Framework Comparison of AI Agents
authors: Soufiane Amini, Yassine Benajiba, Cesare Bernardis, Paul Cayet, Hassan Chafi, Abderrahim Fathan, Louis Faucon, Damien Hilloulin, Sungpack Hong, Ingo Kossyk, Tirthankar Lahiri, Tran Minh Son Le, Rhicheek Patra, Sujith Ravi, Jonas Schweizer, Jyotika Singh, Shailender Singh, Weiyi Sun, Kartik Talamadupula, Jerry Xu
publish: Proceedings of the ACM Conference on AI and Agentic Systems
abstract: None

title: Metrics for Privacy-Preserving Generative Models: A Comprehensive Survey
authors: Debalina Padariya, Isabel Wagner, Aboozar Taherkhani, Eerke A. Boiten
abstract: None

title: Managing Edge Resources at Scale: A Peer-to-Peer CDN for On-Demand Video Streaming
authors: Rui-Xiao Zhang, Haiping Wang, Shu Shi, Xiaofei Pang, Klara Nahrstedt, Jiangchuan Liu
abstract: None

title: Detecting Craters in Images Using Machine Learning and Classical Techniques for Lunar Navigation
authors: Trupti Mahendrakar, Kyle Smith, Davis Adams, Shaurya Gupta, Max Marshall, Jorge Chong, Sofia Catalan
abstract: Loss of ground-based communication is an increasing concern for crewed and uncrewed spacecraft as cislunar traffic grows. To address navigation challenges in lunar environments, a Target & Range-adaptive Optical Navigation system (TRON) was developed to enable onboard navigation independent of ground communication. One component of the TRON suite is a technique designed specifically for terrain relative navigation (TRN) through the identification of lunar craters from orbital altitudes. The effectiveness of this method is directly tied to crater detection performance, as many false positives or false negatives can deteriorate the navigation results downstream. This paper presents a combination of machine learning and traditional computer vision methods for crater identification with built-in fault prevention. The approach is evaluated on synthetic imagery from Gateway's NRHO orbit and real lunar imagery collected during the Artemis I mission, showing promising results and establishing a clear path toward future flight demonstrations.

title: Classification Quality of Air with Real Time IoT Based Air Monitoring Device Analysis
authors: Alya Rafilah Ramli, Rifqi Abdul Hanif, Panglima Abdan Diwan Syakura, Muhammad Fahri Hairul, Andi Seylo Irham Irawan, M. Saleh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Current Research in Artificial Intelligence and Data Science (ICCRAIDS)
abstract: Air pollution remains a major environmental and public health concern, with conventional monitoring systems often limited by high costs, restricted coverage, and slow data reporting. This research develops a high-accuracy real-time air quality (AQ) classification system by integrating Internet of Things (IoT) technology with machine learning (ML) methods, resulting in a scalable, reliable, and cost-efficient framework. Utilizing an ESP32 microcontroller with DHT22, MQ135, and GP2Y1010AU0F sensors, the system measures key environmental parameters and transmits data to a Google Spreadsheet for real-time cloud storage, while also providing instant user notifications via a Telegram Bot. The K-Nearest Neighbor (K-NN) algorithm is employed to classify AQ indices into ‘Good,’ ‘Insufficient,’ or ‘Hazardous’ categories. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed K-NN model achieves a classification accuracy of 96.77%, confirming the effectiveness and reliability of the system as a practical tool for continuous AQ monitoring.

title: Adaptive Speech Enhancement Using a Hybrid CUDA and Deep Learning Pipeline
authors: Anumakonda Vyshnav Kumar, Ron Varghese Sam, Arun M
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Applications Theme: Healthcare and Internet of Things (AIMLA)
abstract: We describe a real-time pipeline for speech enhancement that unites GPU-parallel digital signal processing with a pretrained deep-learning model, targeting noise suppression, voice amplification, and hearing-loss compensation within a single framework. To create test conditions that mirror real urban environments, clean utterances from the LibriSpeech dev-clean subset were mixed with genuine STRAFFIC traffic recordings at input signal-to-noise ratios of 0,5, and 10 dB. Processing occurs in the frequency domain throughout: cuFFT and handwritten CUDA kernels carry out Wiener-style suppression and formant-sensitive amplification over 513 spectral bins for every frame, while a SpeechBrain MetricGAN+ model trained on VoiceBank+DEMAND supplies a high-fidelity denoised signal that enters the GPU gain stage directly. Experiments run on an NVIDIA T4 in Google Colab show that the CUDA-only path raises SNR by roughly +3 to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$+6 \mathbf{d B}$</tex>, whereas the combined ML+CUDA setup achieves gains of + 6 to + <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$12 \mathbf{~ d B}$</tex> over the unprocessed input, with per-frame GPU latency staying between 0.3 and 1.2 ms. The pipeline also accommodates audiogram-derived, per-bin gain curves, confirming that GPU-resident frequencydomain processing is a realistic and practical foundation for personalized, hearing-aid-style enhancement.

title: Re-Evaluation of Solar PV-Battery Sizing Optimization for Cost-Effective Hybrid Microgrids
authors: Pinar Oguz Ekim, Elias Ouedraogo, Erhan Demirok
publish: 2026 16th International Conference on Power, Energy, and Electrical Engineering (CPEEE)
abstract: This paper re-evaluates the cost-optimal sizing of solar photovoltaic and battery systems in hybrid microgrids using full-year simulation data. The objective is to minimize the cost of energy by jointly optimizing solar and battery capacities under realistic load and solar generation profiles. Case studies across five European cities show that hybrid configurations, which allow limited grid interaction, consistently outperform fully renewable systems in terms of cost of energy. Notably, energy costs are reduced by 40% to 70% in hybrid setups, with solar fractions exceeding 90% in high-irradiance locations like Madrid. The results also highlight the economic inefficiency of over-sizing batteries to eliminate PV curtailment. Instead, allowing moderate curtailment results in better overall system economics. Optimal battery durations range between 1.3 and 2.0 hours, questioning the cost-effectiveness of longer-duration storage under current research trends. This study provides actionable insights for designing technically feasible and economically competitive distributed energy systems in rural and semi-urban contexts.

title: A Fine-Tuned ResNet-50 Approach for Enhanced Pneumonia Classification
authors: M Anirudhan, Nikhitha S E, H. N Mandal
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Applications Theme: Healthcare and Internet of Things (AIMLA)
abstract: Pneumonia is a serious global health problem that affects children and the elderly the most. Early diagnosis is very important for lowering death rates First, reading chest X-ray images by hand is time-consuming, and individual approaches to doing it probably vary from one person to the next in resource-limited clinical settings. In this paper we present a hybrid ensemble-based deep learning framework which achieves a high accuracy in pneumonia classification from chest X-ray images. The proposed architecture is a combination of some convolutional neural network architectures, so it can benefit from the advantages of all different architectures and make diagnosis more reliable. Image preprocessing and augmentation methods help to enrich the data for better quality of data and generalization. We use accuracy, precision, recall and F1-score to check how well the model performs. Experimental results show that the ensemble approach outperforms individual models and therefore provides more reliability and consistency.

title: SMARTFLOW: Intelligent Traffic Management with Real-Time Density and Emergency Response
authors: R. Ratna Kowshik, V. Pavani, R. Anandh
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Applications Theme: Healthcare and Internet of Things (AIMLA)
abstract: However, due to the rapid growth of urbanization, there has been an increase in traffic congestion, which causes the inefficient management of the traffic signals and the delayed response to emergencies. In the traditional system of controlling traffic signals, a static plan is used for the timing purpose, which cannot be adapted to the real-time situation. In order to overcome the problems faced in the traditional system, the AI-assisted adaptive traffic signal control system with emergency vehicle prioritization is introduced in this paper. In the proposed system, the video captured from the roadside cameras is used to identify the vehicles using the YOLOv8 model. The vehicles are classified into two-wheelers and four-wheelers. The weighted density is used to identify the congestion on a per-lane basis. Emergency vehicles such as ambulances and fire trucks are identi- fied using visual and auditory signals to provide the right-of-way priority. The entire system is developed using the Python pro- gramming language along with the OpenCV and PyTorch libraries. The proposed system is a highly efficient solution for the modern traffic signals that replaces the traditional static timers with the AI-assisted adaptive solution.

title: Contextual Models for Indonesian Abbreviation Disambiguation and Expansion
authors: Hansen Halim, Aurelia Yap, Lili Ayu Wulandhari, Noviyanti Tri Maretta Sagala
publish: 2026 International Conference on Current Research in Artificial Intelligence and Data Science (ICCRAIDS)
abstract: Abbreviations and acronyms are widely used today in Indonesian news articles and formal writing, unfortunately for machines, their meanings are often context dependent and ambiguous. This paper will talk about the full end to end pipeline for Indonesian abbreviation expansion and disambiguation starting from data collection to evaluation. We first scrape 3,500 Kompas articles and constructed a new abbreviation corpus by automatically extracting abbreviation and definition pair using rule-based search. The pairs were manually validated. We then try out the performance of 4 disambiguation models: 1. A TF-IDF + LR 2. BiLSTM, 3. BiLSTM + TF-IDF, 4. Fine-tuned IndoBERT. The models are evaluated using Top-K accuracy and MRR under two conditions: standard decoding over all possible expansions and candidate-constrained decoding where predictions are restricted to expansions observed for the abbreviation. On the held-out test set, IndoBERT performs best under standard decoding (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$87.0 \% \quad$</tex> Acc <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${@} 1,0.913 \quad$</tex> MRR), substantially outperforming the TF-IDF baseline (66.5 % Acc @ 1, 0.721 MRR). We additionally introduce WSD-style retrieval baselines-an embedding-centroid prototype model and kNN retrieval-which are highly competitive in the candidate-constrained setting and provide a strong non-generative alternative to sequence models. Slice evaluation by ambiguity level shows that performance degrades as the candidate set grows (i.e., more expansions per abbreviation), indicating that genuinely ambiguous cases remain the primary challenge. Finally, error analysis of IndoBERT reveals that remaining mistakes are dominated by unseen abbreviations and named-entity sense confusions, with fewer errors attributable to near-string or near-synonym variants. Overall, these results suggest that contextual transformers are most beneficial for highly ambiguous abbreviations, while retrieval/lexical baselines are strong when candidate expansions are known, providing a foundation for Indonesian abbreviation processing and supporting downstream tasks such as machine translation and information extraction.

title: Entity Resolution for Aircraft Type Matching on Heterogeneous Aviation Data Sources
authors: Karna Bryan, Irene Tsapara
abstract: The lack of standardization in aircraft type identification across diverse aviation databases hinders safety analysis by creating barriers to data integration. Studies that aggregate data by aircraft type are time-consuming and require substantial manual cleaning due to the absence of common keys, inconsistent representations, and varying levels of granularity, including multiple versions of make-model-series, International Civil Aviation Organization (ICAO) codes, and popular or marketing names. These inconsistencies make it difficult to align safety event records with operational exposure metrics, complicating risk calculations that rely on linking event counts (numerators) with exposure data (denominators) stored in separate and often incompatible systems. This research applies the entity resolution (ER) framework to the aviation domain, introducing a novel benchmark for evaluating ER techniques. ER enables automated matching of records across datasets lacking common identifiers by leveraging similarities in attribute content and structure. A key contribution is the development of a hierarchical, hand-labeled “gold” dataset aligning aircraft types across two aviation taxonomies, serving as a benchmark for training and evaluation. Both a baseline feature-based model and a deep learning model using the opensource DITTO framework are developed. DITTO employs Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers (BERT)-based sequence-pair encodings with a fine-tuned binary classifier. Results show that the deep learning model performs the taxonomy-to-taxonomy matching with high performance (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F}_{1} \boldsymbol{=} \mathbf{0. 9 8}$</tex>) and demonstrates potential for scalable, automated aircraft type resolution across heterogeneous aviation datasets.

title: A Python-Based Framework for Integrating Multi-Modal AI in Automated Video Production Workflows: A Strategic Business Perspective
authors: Antonius Felix, Julius Sutrisno, Selly Swandari, Devi Yurisca Bernanda, Agung Stefanus Kembau, Vincentius Leonardo
publish: 2026 International Conference on Current Research in Artificial Intelligence and Data Science (ICCRAIDS)
abstract: The exponential growth of video consumption has outpaced traditional, labor-intensive production workflows, creating a need for scalable solutions. This research introduces a Python-based framework integrating multi-modal AI including LLM-powered scripting, text-to-video synthesis, and voice cloning to automate end-to-end production. Systematic deployment across e-commerce, education, and marketing sectors reveals transformative results. Empirical data shows a 73% reduction in production time (from 120 to 32 minutes) and an 85 % decrease in unit costs (from Rp70,000 to <Rp10,000). Furthermore, the framework achieved a 267 % increase in throughput and a 19 % improvement in quality consistency. Beyond operational metrics, the study demonstrates that AI-driven automation democratizes high-volume content strategies previously reserved for resource-rich competitors. However, the findings emphasize that technical deployment must be supported by comprehensive change management and workforce upskilling to succeed. Ultimately, as AI capabilities advance, video production automation is transitioning from a competitive differentiator to a strategic necessity. Organizations failing to adopt these frameworks risk significant capability gaps in an increasingly automated digital landscape.

title: Physics-Informed Neural Networks for Nonlinear Model Predictive Control: A Comprehensive Framework with Evolutionary Optimization
authors: Kiruba R, Boobalan J, M. Saranya, Sumitharaj R
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Applications Theme: Healthcare and Internet of Things (AIMLA)
abstract: Physics-Informed Neural Networks (PINNs) are a potential paradigm for solving large differential equations that incorporate controlling physics into neural structures. Leveraging this capability, an integrated framework that unites PINNs with Nonlinear Model Predictive Control (NMPC) is proposed to improve the regulation of nonlinear dynamical systems. A key innovation lies in replacing conventional gradient-based training, which is prone to spectral bias and local minima, with an evolutionary optimization strategy that enhances convergence reliability and model stability. This hybrid learning-control approach merges data-driven approximation with physics-guided constraints, yielding predictive models that are both accurate and physically consistent. The framework is validated on a mass-springdamper benchmark, where the PINN-enhanced NMPC demonstrates faster convergence, better generalization across varying conditions, and improved control precision compared to standard methods. Importantly, the reduced computational overhead enables real-time trajectory tracking, highlighting the practicality of the approach. By linking machine learning with physics-informed control, this work establishes a pathway toward scalable and intelligent control architectures for advanced engineering systems.

title: Inverse Model Predictive Control for Bidirectional Power Flow in Wireless EV Charging Systems
authors: M. Álvarez-Duque, W.J. Gil-González, A. Escobar-Mejía
publish: 2026 16th International Conference on Power, Energy, and Electrical Engineering (CPEEE)
abstract: Wireless power transfer (WPT) systems based on inductive coupling have emerged as a promising solution for battery charging in energy storage applications and electric mobility. Most existing studies on WPT systems have primarily focused on unidirectional power flow, primarily addressing battery charging operation. In contrast, bidirectional power transfer-enabling both charging and discharging functionalities-has received comparatively less attention. Recently, bidirectional dc-dc converter topologies, particularly the dual active bridge (DAB), have begun to be explored in WPT applications due to their inherent bidirectional power flow capability, galvanic isolation, modular structure and flexible phase-shift-based control. However, conventional control strategies for DAB-based WPT systems, such as PI phase delay control and self-Tuning control, exhibit sensitivity to coupling and load variations, causing implementation complexity and a large number of tuning parameters; on the other hand classical Model Predictive Control (MPC) entails a high computational burden. This paper presents the design and validation of an Inverse Model Predictive Control (IMPC) strategy for a DAB-based WPT system aimed to regulate the dc output current during battery charging and discharging. The proposed controller is compared with a conventional PI controller. Time-domain simulations will be carried out using MATLAB/Simulink environment and is subsequently validated experimentally using a low-scale prototype. The results obtained from both simulation and experimental evaluations demonstrate that the IMPC controller improves the dynamic response by reducing the settling time and minimizing the signal's steady state error.

title: Physical Maxillofacial Simulator with Variable-Stiffness Sensorized Soft Tissues
authors: Alessio De Bona, Alberto Villani, Domenico Prattichizzo, Gionata Salvietti
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th International Conference on Soft Robotics (RoboSoft)
abstract: Realistic physical simulators for maxillofacial surgeons are still limited due to the lack of soft-tissue models capable of reproducing the complex mechanical behavior of human facial anatomy. This work presents a novel physical maxillofacial simulator specifically designed for diagnostic palpation. It integrates a high-fidelity 3D-printed skull with sensorized, pneumatic soft pads. The latter can actively modulate stiffness and shape to emulate both physiological and pathological tissue conditions. Each pad reproduces the layered structure of selected facial regions, i.e., mandibular, zygomatic, and maxillary. They are fabricated from silicone-based materials optimized to match human tissue compliance. Moreover, the system records palpation forces and dynamically varies internal pressure to simulate edematous or fibrotic responses. Experimental characterization demonstrates repeatable inflation–pressure dynamics, nonlinear stiffness modulation, and anatomically consistent swelling behavior. Validation with a maxillofacial surgeon confirms the simulator’s anatomical realism, tactile fidelity, and training effectiveness. Therefore, the proposed platform bridges the gap between static anatomical phantoms and virtual haptic systems, providing a controllable and high-fidelity environment for surgical training and diagnostic skill assessment.

title: Locational Marginal Pricing (LMP) Analysis in a Two-Settlement System Using PyPSA
authors: Samiksha Koirala, Xin Wang
publish: 2026 16th International Conference on Power, Energy, and Electrical Engineering (CPEEE)
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive analysis of Locational Marginal Pricing (LMP) within a two-settlement electricity market using the IEEE 14 -bus test system modeled in PyPSA. The study simulates both the Day-Ahead Market (DAM) and Real-Time Market (RTM) using DC Optimal Power Flow (DC-OPF) to evaluate the economic and operational impacts of transmission congestion and power losses. LMP is decomposed into Marginal Energy Cost (MEC), Marginal Loss Component (MLC), and Marginal Congestion Component (MCC). Additional metrics such as Generation Shift Factors (GSF), loss sensitivities, and flowgate shadow prices are computed to assess grid flexibility and investment signals. Results show significant LMP variation due to congestion, especially in buses distant from the slack generator. The system demonstrates how open-source modeling tools can support transparent, nodal-level market analysis, enabling better planning and real-time decision-making. Future extensions include AC-OPF modeling, stochastic demand scenarios, and distributed resource integration.

title: Design Methodology and Modeling of Matrix-Based Magnetically Integrated Resonant Inductors for Server Power Applications
authors: Yu-Chen Liu, Shang-Syun Wu, Sheng-Chieh Lan
publish: 2026 16th International Conference on Power, Energy, and Electrical Engineering (CPEEE)
abstract: In recent years, the rapid expansion of highperformance computing platforms and data-center systems has driven a sustained increase in power demand, placing strict requirements on the efficiency and power density of isolated DCDC converters. In large step-down applications supplied by highvoltage DC buses, magnetic component design has emerged as a primary factor limiting further improvement in overall system performance. This work develops a structured design methodology for a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.5 ~\text{kW}, 390 / 12 ~\mathrm{V}$</tex> LLC resonant converter. The resonant tank parameters are determined through analytical consideration of the coupled effects of dead time, resonant current, and voltage gain, enabling a reduction in circulating energy and overall loss while preserving the required gain characteristics. A matrix-type resonant inductor is employed to improve high frequency magnetic flux distribution and to reduce AC winding loss associated with magnetomotive force (MMF) accumulation, proximity effect, and current sharing. Finite element analysis is used to characterize leakage inductance, AC resistance (ACR), and electromagnetic field behavior, with particular attention to the influence of PCB via configuration on current sharing among parallel windings and its impact on effective inductance and copper loss. Experimental results obtained from a hardware prototype validate the feasibility of the proposed resonant tank and magnetic design approach, demonstrating stable electrical performance and confirming the effectiveness of the matrix-type resonant inductor in high-power LLC applications.

title: From Biometric Surfaces to Contextual Intelligence: A Comprehensive Survey of Multimodal Authentication and the Trimodal Fusion Paradigm
authors: Dummy. Nurdin, Armin Lawi, Syamsuddin Toaha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Current Research in Artificial Intelligence and Data Science (ICCRAIDS)
abstract: The rapid advancement of Generative AI-based cyberattacks and deepfakes has exposed the inherent vulnerabilities of unimodal biometric systems. This article presents a systematic and comprehensive survey of multimodal authentication, evolving from traditional physical biometrics to a “Trimodal Fusion” paradigm that integrates physiological (face), behavioral (voice), and contextual intelligence (location). Through a rigorous review of literature from 2008 to 2025, we map the technological transition from classical statistical methods, such as Gaussian Mixture Models (GMM), to adaptive Deep Learning (DL) architectures utilizing attention mechanisms. Our analysis identifies a critical security paradigm shift toward a Zero Trust model. We propose a novel HybridSequential Architecture that prioritizes a Pre-Fusion Validation Layer incorporating liveness detection via internal modalities (e.g., ECG) and data integrity checks before feature-level integration. A unique contribution of this survey is the conceptualization of the “Location Trust Anchor,” which utilizes spatial context to dynamically adjust security thresholds while mitigating privacy risks through Deep Hashing and Blockchain integration. Furthermore, we explore the dual role of Generative AI, identifying it not only as a sophisticated threat vector but also as a defensive mechanism for missing modality recovery. This study concludes by addressing the implementation feasibility of edge devices and providing a roadmap for future research in privacy-preserving, resilient multimodal authentication ecosystems.

title: AI-Driven Consciousness Assessment, Recovery Forecasting & Hydration Management for Coma Patients
authors: T. Joby Titus, Reenasri S, Sadhana L, Sujaaya J
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Applications Theme: Healthcare and Internet of Things (AIMLA)
abstract: Coma patients require continuous monitoring and timely medical intervention in intensive care units (ICU). Such patients need to be regularly and even continuously monitored, quickly intervened when the need arises, and, on time, accurately diagnosed to know the prognosis. Hence, the authors in the paper suggest an AI-based healthcare system that can determine the level of consciousness in real-time, predict recovery, and manage the hydration of coma patients. This system which is under the spotlight in the paper uses a set of biomedical sensors to record the patient's physiological signals such as EEG, heart rate, oxygen saturation, and hydration level. These physiological data are transferred through an IoT infrastructure to the cloud where machine learning models analyse the data for prediction. Patient consciousness interpretation, as well as recovery prediction, are performed by the K-Nearest Neighbours (KNN) classifier. The hydration monitoring module provides timely alerts to prevent dehydration-related complications. The experiment results have confirmed that the consciousness manifestation detection's precision reaches 88 %, the recovery prediction performance corresponds to a value of 0.81 for the area under the ROC curve, and the hydration accuracy is 92 %. On top of that, the average response time to an alert is only 1.2 s. The results indicate that the system presented here significantly supports the work of clinicians in making decisions and, thus, gives a great boost to personalized critical care.

title: Low-Control Satellite Formations for Enhanced GNSS-R Resolution in Perturbed Orbits
authors: Angel Pan Du, Lawanya Awasthi, M. Amin Alandihallaj, Andreas M. Hein
abstract: High-fidelity Earth observations for measuring geophysical parameters have become indispensable for areas such as climate science and resource management. In recent years, this industry has driven a shift toward formations of small and cost-effective satellites rather than single large platforms. These distributed systems enable simultaneous, multi-point measurements across wide areas, improving temporal coverage and resilience. Global Navigation Satellite System Reflectometry (GNSS-R) exploits navigation signals reflected from the Earth surface to retrieve geophysical parameters, and provides a low-cost and resilient approach for Earth observation, but is constrained by coarse spatial resolution, typically above 10 km. This study investigates a novel concept to overcome this limitation by employing a synchronized CubeSat formation that synthesizes a large aperture through beamforming, enabling sub-kilometer resolution. A comprehensive framework is developed to analyze the relationship between formation geometry, beamforming performance, and station-keeping requirements under orbital perturbations. The methodology combines Clohessy-Wiltshire-based relative motion modeling, perturbation analysis including Earth oblateness, and beamforming simulations to evaluate spatial resolution and sidelobe performance. A multivariable optimization explores trade-offs between resolution, ground-illumination stability, and formation complexity. The results indicate that spiral formations provide favorable performance, with a configuration of 29 satellites achieving near-optimal resolution while minimizing formation size. However, perturbations such as <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$J_{2}$</tex> induce drift that degrades resolution beyond 1 km after a few orbits, highlighting the need for efficient formation-keeping strategies. Given the constraints of CubeSats, including limited volume, mass and power, propellant-less control methods, such as differential drag or solar radiation pressure, emerge as promising solutions for enabling long-duration, scalable missions within CubeSat constraints. These findings demonstrate the feasibility of distributed GNSS-R missions with significantly improved spatial resolution and lay the foundation for future work on control strategies and end-to-end mission design, enabling new applications in fine-scale Earth observation.

title: DIGSAFE: A 5G-Enabled Smart Helmet for Worker Safety in Hazardous Environments
authors: Pradeep D, Arun Sanjeev M S, Aswanth K, Ganesh R, Kumar Mohit M
publish: 2026 International Conference on Current Trends in Advanced Computing (ICCTAC)
abstract: Workers in hazardous workplaces like construction sites and mining firms are exposed to hazards like toxic gases, extreme temperatures, and unexpected health complications. Conventional safety helmets are passive protection devices and cannot monitor environmental or physiological conditions on a real-time basis. To address this limitation, this paper introduces a 5G-based smart helmet, DIGSAFE, that is equipped with multi-modal sensors and Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) to support proactive worker safety, which make the detection more reliable. The proposed system includes sensors that will be used to detect the level of gases, temperature, humidity, heart rate, and movement, which will allow the constant collection and analysis of data. A 5G communication module will guarantee rapid, low-latency communication of data to a centralized monitoring system that will assist in fast reaction, and life-saving. XAI can be used to generate explainable alerts to enable the system to give clear justification of risks identified and to enhance user trust. Moreover, there is an SOS option that allows workers to send a distress signal in case of an emergency. The system can detect hazardous conditions and issue real-time alerts, as experimental validation shows that the system can detect hazardous conditions accurately. Through its sensing, communication, and explainable intelligence, DIGSAFE transforms the safety management process to a reactive process to a proactive and predictive model. The proposed system provides a scalable and reliable solution to improving the safety of workers working in dangerous conditions.

title: AI-Powered Smart Farming System
authors: C. Edwin Singh, G. Vijitha, G. Eshwar Prasad, A. Udayakumar, M. Saravanan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Current Trends in Advanced Computing (ICCTAC)
abstract: However, modern agricultural practices require intelligent decision support systems to increase agricultural productivity, reduce financial risks, and prevent crop losses. This paper proposes a unified Artificial Intelligence (AI)-based agricultural advisory framework to support crop planning, financial estimation, and crop disease management. The proposed framework integrates various components of AI, including crop planning, seasonal prediction, investment-oriented profit estimation, and deep learning-based crop disease classification, in a unified architecture. The proposed framework has been trained on a structured data set, including various crop, soil, and seasonal characteristics, to train various supervised learning models for crop planning, where Random Forest has been found to have higher classification accuracy. The proposed framework has been evaluated on a regression-based model for estimating crop income by analyzing cultivation expenses and price trends. Additionally, a Convolutional Neural Network has been proposed and trained on a large-scale leaf image data set, including various crop diseases, which has demonstrated high validation accuracy and strong generalization capabilities. The proposed framework, which integrates various agricultural, climatic, financial, and image-based models in a unified architecture, has been evaluated for its effectiveness in real-world agricultural applications.

title: SecuGRC: A Machine Learning Secure SDLC Code Compliance Auditor Based on Governance Framework Mapping
authors: Harshini T.V, Adina Auldwin Paul, B. Uma Maheswari
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Applications Theme: Healthcare and Internet of Things (AIMLA)
abstract: Access authorization failures continue to be a leading source of security failures in enterprises, often because of the disconnect between the officially stated access control policies and their real-world implementation. Traditional Role-Based Access Control audit and checklist-based compliance verification methods do not offer detailed visibility of attribute level breaches and incident based policy deviation. The research paper presents an incident based Attribute-Based Access Control (ABAC) policy auditing and compliance validation model that is aimed at reinforcing safe Software Development Life Cycle governance by formal representation of each access request in terms of subject, object, action and contextual environment parameters, and then by deterministic rule assessment with regard to pre-defined ABAC policy constraints and IT General Control mappings. Access attributes based on incidents are methodically matched with organizational definitions of control to reveal overprivileges, unfinished policy restrictions and inconsistencies in control and policy disparities. To prioritize access violations on the attribute level, a quantified policy deviation severity index is computed. Remediation advice and traceability matrix between incidents, attributes, and access policies and governance controls are automatically produced and meet audit compliance criteria and 94.6 % policy gap detection and 92.1 % remediation recommendation accuracy with experimental validation against enterprise-scale synthetic access datasets. The outcome of the evaluation proves that audit transparency is improved, access governance visibility is improved, and regulatory interpretability is improved in contrast to the use of RBAC-based compliance validation methods. Results prove incident-based ABAC policy auditing to be a powerful, regulator-congruent tool of diminishing access control risk in secure SDLC settings.

title: HoloQRam: Efficient Real-Time Spatial Video Delivery via Animated QR Codes
authors: Jaewon Choi, JeongGil Ko
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Communications (PerCom)
abstract: Public displays remain limited to flat, network-dependent media, missing the opportunities for spontaneous immersive user interaction. In this work, we present HoloQRam, a system for real-time, infrastructure-free spatial video delivery using animated QR codes and mobile RGB-D reconstruction. HoloQRam transmits lightweight 128×128 RGB streams encoded into sequential QR symbols on commodity displays, avoiding device pairing or network connectivity. On the receiver side, a genetic algorithm (GA)-optimized filtering pipeline enhances decoding robustness under various QR image capture conditions, while a hybrid Transformer-CNN jointly performs 4× super-resolution and depth estimation to reconstruct high-quality RGB-D videos. Implemented on smartphones and tested across LCD, OLED, and projector displays, HoloQRam achieves up to 70% higher decoding success rates than baselines, maintains >30 fps throughput, and delivers 30.8 dB PSNR with consistent depth accuracy. Our results demonstrate the feasibility of spontaneous, one-to-many spatial video broadcasting, transforming ordinary public screens into rich volumetric media sharing portals.

title: V2G Operation Strategy for Electric Bus Stations in Multi-Stage Markets
authors: Zhongguang Yang, Weipeng Liang, Yang Du, Lingyu Guo, Simin Wu, Jicheng Hui, Yangguang Lu
publish: 2026 11th Asia Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering (ACPEE)
abstract: To fully exploit the energy storage potential of electric buses (EBs) and maximize their electricity market value, this paper proposes a multi-market coordination strategy enabling EB stations to participate in both energy and frequency regulation markets. A comprehensive operational framework integrating day-ahead, intra-day, and real-time stages is first established. By collecting real-time EB data and considering individual constraints alongside fleet aggregation characteristics, the dispatchable power boundary of the bus cluster is precisely determined. A multi-stage optimization model is then developed to maximize total station profit, incorporating energy procurement costs, frequency regulation capacity revenue, and mileage compensation. Simulation results across four seasons demonstrate that the strategy achieves the highest total profit in autumn, followed by summer, with spring slightly lower and winter the lowest due to battery degradation. This approach effectively balances EB charging demands with grid regulation requirements, supporting multi-product market participation.

title: HoSig-Align I: Edge-Native Threat Attribution using Homology Blocks in IoT-Pervasive Networks
authors: Aiting Yao, Shantanu Pal, Chengzu Dong, Di Shao, Wenying Feng, Ruonan Li, Zhaoquan Gu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Communications (PerCom)
abstract: A primary challenge in network defense is to mine potential attack campaigns from massive, continuously arriving alerts and telemetry data in real time. This paper presents HoSig-Align I, an edge side unsupervised method designed for network streams to discover homologous blocks. Our approach innovatively fuses heterogeneous features like Internet Protocol (IP) and Payload into a unified representation while strictly excluding temporal information from it, only incorporating time via a dual time scale decay model during graph construction to capture temporal proximity. A density robust similarity is computed using an isolation style random partition forest, leading to a sparse k-Nearest Neighbors (k-NN) graph. The stream is then accurately segmented into internally cohesive and mutually isolated homologous blocks through spectral ordering and contrastive change point detection. Each block is encoded into a lightweight HoSig signature, forming the basis for cross organizational collaboration. Experiments on real network streams show that HoSig-Align I identifies coherent attack campaign blocks and improves separation and boundary clarity over baselines, while meeting low-latency and low-overhead requirements for edge processing.

title: Enhanced Stability of DFIG-Based Wind Systems using a Hybrid Sliding-Backstepping Control
authors: Abdallaoui Hasnaa, Dib Faiza, Benaya Nabil, Benmeziane Khaddouj
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI Innovations and Industry (ICAIII)
abstract: This paper proposes a robust hybrid control strategy that combines Sliding Mode Control (SMC) and Backstepping Control for a Doubly Fed Induction Generator (DFIG) used in a wind energy conversion system. The proposed approach aims to reduce the chattering phenomenon and improve the stability, tracking performance, and robustness in the presence of parameter uncertainties and external disturbances. The backstepping technique ensures a systematic controller design with guaranteed stability, while the sliding mode enhances overall system robustness and performance. The proposed control strategy is implemented in the rotor-side converter to achieve independent control of active and reactive power. The simulation results validate the efficiency of the proposed robust hybrid control strategy in achieving a superior dynamic response, smaller steadystate error, and robust performance compared to the existing control strategies, especially under varying wind speeds and system disturbances.

title: Enhancing IoT Security with Optimized Machine Learning Models for Malicious Traffic Detection
authors: Abdulrhman I. AlSahafi
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI Innovations and Industry (ICAIII)
abstract: The rapid expansion of Internet of Things (IoT) ecosystems has introduced significant cybersecurity challenges, particularly in detecting malicious traffic within complex and dynamic network environments. This study proposes a framework for enhancing malicious traffic detection using machine learning (ML) algorithms optimized through Tree-structured Parzen Estimator (TPE). The M-En dataset (a comprehensive resource encapsulating diverse IoT communication patterns) serves as the foundation for experimentation. The methodology encompasses rigorous data pre-processing, including imputation, categorical encoding, and feature scaling, followed by hyperparameter optimization to identify the most effective ML configurations. Evaluation metrics (including precision, recall, F1-score, accuracy, and specificity) are utilized to evaluate model performance. Experimental results demonstrate that Histogram Gradient Boosting (HGB), paired with Recursive Feature Elimination (RFE) and Standard scaling, achieves superior performance, with an average accuracy of 98.48 % and an F 1 -score of 98.07 %. Statistical analyses (including swarm and violin plots) confirm the robustness and consistency of the proposed approach across multiple trials. Comparative evaluations reveal significant improvements over baseline methods, highlighting the framework's potential for realworld IoT cybersecurity applications.

title: The Power of AI Literacy in Developing Students' Innovative Behavior and Wellbeing in Tourism and Hospitality Education: The Roles of Creative Self-Efficacy and Learning Agility
authors: Sarah Abdelrahman, Yehia Elzek, Abdelaziz Aborya
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI Innovations and Industry (ICAIII)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) literacy has become one of the essential competencies in higher education due to its positive influence on students' creativity, adaptability, and overall wellbeing. This research aims to explore the role of AI literacy in enhancing students' innovative behavior and wellbeing within tourism and hospitality education. The study also seeks to examine the mediating role of creative self- efficacy in the relationship between AI literacy, students' innovative behavior, and wellbeing. In addition, the research aims to identify the moderating effect of learning agility on the relationships between creative self-efficacy, innovative behavior, and wellbeing. To achieve these objectives, the research depended on the quantitative approach. A structured electronic questionnaire developed and distributed to a sample of tourism and hospitality students in Egypt and Saudi Arabia. The data collected was analyzed using WarpPLS V. 8 to test the hypothesized relationships among the study variables. The findings revealed that AI literacy has a significant positive effect on students' innovative behavior and wellbeing. Creative selfefficacy plays a key mediating role in the relationships between AI literacy, innovative behavior, and wellbeing, while students' innovative behavior further mediates the link between creative self-efficacy and wellbeing. Most hypothesized relationships were supported, whereas the moderating effect of learning agility was not significant. Based on the findings, a set of recommendations proposed to help tourism and hospitality education effectively integrate AI literacy and develop agile learning environments that support creativity, innovation, and student wellbeing in the era of digital transformation.

title: AI-Enhanced Color Contrast Checker for WCAG Oriented Real-Time Accessibility Evaluation
authors: Danish Mansoor Khan, Zohaib Shahid, Amina Khan
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI Innovations and Industry (ICAIII)
abstract: Availability on online platforms has become more troublesome as the new web applications are fitted with complex user interfaces that are visually involved. The most common accessibility issue is the lack of adequate colour contrast, which is a violation of the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG), causing a negative impact on the readability, usability, and compliance with regulations by people with visual impairments. In this paper, TheColorContrastChecker, an artificial intelligence-based web application, is presented that is capable of evaluating and refining colour-contrast accessibility in real time in accordance with the WCAG 2.1 and 2.2 standards. The system compiles the contrast ratios based on the WCAG relative luminance formula and checks the compliance of AA and AAA. In contrast to traditional solutions, which offer a binary pass/fail result, the proposed solution incorporates a client-side TensorFlow.js model, which then gives WCAG-based colour suggestions through smart alterations of brightness and saturation without compromising the brand identity. The tool also facilitates live interface previews as well as accessibility guidance in a low-latency workflow. Inference latency of the order of near-instant deterministic validation and sub- 200 ms AI inference is shown by the experimental results of the live platform, which makes it smooth for real-time remediation. On selected case studies, the system proved its excellence as it was able to raise the ratios of contrast previously below standard (such as 2.82 to 8.08) without losing the necessary brand-specific colors. This paper demonstrates that client-side artificial intelligence changes traditional accessibility testing into a context-sensitive and interactive design assistant.

title: IoT Based Integrated Design for Aeroponic Plant Cultivation Using LSTM Algorithm
authors: K.R. Gokul Anand, P. Dhivya, D. Faridha Banu, Boopathy S, M. Sivakumar, Dinesh E
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Applications Theme: Healthcare and Internet of Things (AIMLA)
abstract: To sustain the increasing global population, there is a need for new and innovative agricultural approaches that can minimize the negative impact of shortages in natural resources and environmental degradation, while ensuring that the basic human need of food security is met. Traditional soil-based agriculture is becoming unsustainable because of various factors such as soil erosion, over-irrigation, loss of fertilizers from the soil, and declining freshwater. As an alternative, aeroponic farming is a new and innovative growing process that is not resource dependent, provides greater crop yields, allows for more efficient use of natural resources, and is less susceptible to attacks from pests and diseases, as well as climate conditions. However, due to the lack of a reliable irrigation system, there can frequently be too much or too little water applied, resulting in poor growth and production of the plants. Recent research has used machine learning algorithms to monitor the health of the plant and to make decisions on when to irrigate; however, the accuracy of those predictions have been very low due to the inability of those algorithms to make long-term time-based correlations and to adapt to changing environmental conditions. In this study, a solution to these problems is proposed with the integration of an IoTenabled integrated aeroponic growing system using Long Short Term Memory (LSTM) networks. By utilizing IoT architecture to collect real-time measurements of environmental parameters including temperature, humidity, CO2 levels, and amount of light, various methods will enable prediction of the amount of water needed for irrigation and how fast plants are photosynthesizing with LSTM models. This is accomplished by linking these various forms of technology, allowing for efficient use of resources and increasing productivity per acre.

title: Automated Debris Localization in Water Bodies Using Feature Mapping
authors: N. Giridharan, S. V. Dhananjay, J. Rahul, J. K. Shalini
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Applications Theme: Healthcare and Internet of Things (AIMLA)
abstract: Debris floating on water surfaces presents major environmental issues, and manual monitoring techniques are ineffective, require much labor, and are not suitable for widespread use. This study introduces a method utilizing a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) for identifying and pinpointing floating debris through RGB images of water surfaces. The suggested system combines image preprocessing, convolutional feature extraction, classification, and bounding-box localization to precisely detect debris areas. The model undergoes evaluation using a dataset of water surface images gathered under diverse environmental conditions, which encompass variations in lighting, reflections, and water textures. Experimental findings show that the suggested method enhances detection accuracy from 0.78 to 0.89 and increases the F1-score from 0.74 to 0.87 in comparison to conventional image processing methods. The streamlined architecture guarantees lower computational complexity while preserving dependable detection capabilities, rendering the system appropriate for real-time environmental observation and implementation on budget-friendly hardware platforms like drones and embedded systems.

title: Cover Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 11th Asia Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering (ACPEE)

title: The Design and Performance of the BAE Systems FASTER Hyperspectral Microwave Radiometer
authors: David Newell, David Draper, Benjamin Davis, John Galantowicz, Allison Zapalac, Pan Liang
abstract: BAE Systems is conducting the R1 mission on the International Space Station (ISS) which includes a hyperspectral radiometer named the Full Atmosphere Sounding Radiometer (FASTER). The R1 mission is a BAE Systems activity to develop and demonstrate a minisat/CubeSat-compatible hyperspectral radiometer and advance the technology readiness level (TRL) of this hardware through space operation.
