This work relies on classifying hadiths according to the analysis of the textual content of (Maten)into four categories: Sahih, Hasan, Daif, and Mawdu. We used several pre-trained Arabic language models, including CAMeLBERT, MARBERT, ArabicBERT, QARiBERT, AraBERT, and AraGPT2. The results showed that CAMeLBERT and QARiBERT achieved the highest performance, with an accuracy of 0.94.

title: AI-Driven Adaptive Cooling Control for Thermal Management of Electric Vehicle Power Electronics
authors: Shanthababu Pandian, Sampath P S, Shivakumar R, Sasikumar A, Sri Maha Vishnu R K, Karan M M, Vivekanandhan Chinnasamy
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Applications Theme: Healthcare and Internet of Things (AIMLA)
abstract: The traditional internal combustion engine produce smoke and cause environmental hazards for long term and extraction of fossil fuels cause depletion of resource on earth. To address these challenge, researchers and industries have explored various energy sources for transportation. Among explored energy sources EV have showcased promising future as alternative energy resource. Electronic vehicles power components exhibit significant amount of heat. Traditional cooling systems are capable to reduce heat generation.Reducing heat generation after reaching threshold temperature is not efficient way. Our paper proposes ai driven predictive cooling system, which can sense approaching critical temperature and start cooling system to reduce the temperature rise.

title: A Hybrid Fault Tree-Markov Chain Approach for Dynamic Fault Risk Prediction of Active Distribution Networks Under Typhoon Events
authors: Bude Su, Lei Chen, Hongkun Chen, Yi Zheng, Yifei Li, Xiaoxuan Guo
publish: 2026 11th Asia Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering (ACPEE)
abstract: With the increasing frequency and intensity of typhoon events, the safe and stable operation of active distribution networks (ADNs) with distributed renewable energy sources (RESs) and energy storage units (ESUs) is facing unprecedented challenges. This paper proposes a novel dynamic fault risk prediction method for ADNs under typhoon events using a hybrid fault tree-Markov chain (FT-MC) model. First, a typhoon wind-field model is established to quantify the time-varying environmental loads, accounting for the spatiotemporal inhomogeneity of typhoon disturbances. Based on this, multi-state time-varying failure-rate models for key components, including overhead lines and photovoltaic (PV) systems, are formulated, along with a multi-state operation constraint model for ESUs. Then, a system-level fault tree is constructed with system power supply failure explicitly defined as the top event to clarify the logical propagation path from component faults to system outages. Furthermore, the fault tree is mapped onto a continuous-time Markov chain state-transition model to dynamically calculate the temporal evolution of the system failure probability. Finally, case studies are conducted on the IEEE 33-bus system, and the results show that the proposed method significantly outperforms the conventional static failure rate (TSFR) method in characterizing the system fault risk, identifying core high-risk components, and improving the distinguishability of system risk levels. The effectiveness and superiority of the proposed fault risk prediction method are well validated.

title: An Efficient Predictive Scoring-Based Method for DFA Construction from Input Strings
authors: V. Vijayalakshmi, D. Sivaganesan
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Applications Theme: Healthcare and Internet of Things (AIMLA)
abstract: Deterministic Finite Automata (DFA) are widely used in applications such as pattern recognition, compiler design, lexical analysis, and text processing. However, classical DFA construction methods often suffer from state explosion and sensitivity to noisy input sequences. This paper proposes PSM-DFA, a predictive scoring-guided state merging approach for efficient DFA construction from labeled input strings. The proposed framework first constructs a Prefix Tree Acceptor (PTA) from input sequences and then applies a structured predictive scoring mechanism to guide state merging decisions. The scoring model evaluates candidate merges using transition similarity, prefix-suffix structural patterns, and conflict penalties to prevent incorrect merges while controlling state growth. Experimental evaluation on binary string datasets, palindromic patterns, and protocol-like sequences demonstrates that PSM-DFA produces more compact DFAs compared to classical state merging approaches while maintaining high classification accuracy. Furthermore, under simulated noisy input conditions, the proposed method maintains robust performance with accuracy above 85%. The results indicate that predictive scoring-guided merging provides an interpretable and efficient strategy for practical DFA construction tasks in pattern recognition and language processing applications.

title: Event Reconstruction From Forensic Timeline with Formal Event Modeling
authors: Hudan Studiawan, Immanuel M. T. Pardede, Asanka P. Sayakkara, Baskoro A. Pratomo, Ary M. Shiddiqi
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI Innovations and Industry (ICAIII)
abstract: Digital forensic investigations require structured methods to analyze heterogeneous system logs and reconstruct accurate timelines of user activities. This study presents a lightweight formal model for the reconstruction of digital forensic events using extraction, mapping, and analysis operators. The method transforms heterogeneous web and system logs into unified entities, relations, and a filtered event timeline. The method applies temporal correlation to distinguish meaningful user actions from automated background processes. It reveals clear behavioral phases such as browser activities, software installation, PowerShell execution, and system shutdown. Experiments with multiple forensic case studies show that the model consistently isolates high-relevance events, with low fitness and generalization scores that indicate its deliberate focus on evidence-driven reconstruction.

title: Equations over Finite Monoids with Infinite Promises
authors: Alberto Larrauri, Antoine Mottet, Stanislav Živný
abstract: None

title: Safeguarding Low-Altitude Integrated Sensing and Communications Networks via UAV-Mounted RIS
authors: Shanza Shakoor, Quang Nhat Le, Trung Q. Duong
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Signal Processing
abstract: In this paper, we investigate secure transmission in an integrated sensing and communication-cell-free network with the help of a low-altitude uncrewed aerial vehicle-mounted reconfigurable intelligent surface (URIS). The dual-functional access points (APs) simultaneously transmit communication signals to serve legitimate users and emit sensing signal to sense the target, who also acts as a potential eavesdropper (Eve) to intercept the communication signals. To enhance security, the artificial noise (AN) is employed by APs to degrade the Eve's reception. We formulate a sum secrecy rate maximization problem that jointly optimizes the APs' transmit beamformings, AN covariance, RIS's phase shifts, and URIS trajectory. Due to the non-convex nature of the formulated problem, it is divided into three tractable subproblems and solved by an iterative alternating optimization algorithm that incorporates the majorization–minimization framework, semidefinite relaxation, and successive convex approximation techniques. Numerical results confirm that the proposed scheme significantly improves secrecy performance compared with baseline schemes without using AN, fixed RIS, and without deploying URIS.

title: Copyright and Reprint permission
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Machine Learning, and Intelligence (DataSciMI)

title: Photoconductive Semiconductor Switches: Uncertainty in the Material Properties
authors: Timothy Wolfe, Alejandro Strachan
abstract: The ambiguity surrounding key optical and electronic material properties in wide band gap semiconductors presents a significant obstacle to further advancing the state of the art in Photoconductive Semiconductor Switches (PCSS) development and integration. Most significantly, the ionization potentials of optically active crystal defects remain uncertain across a range of reported measurement results, with indications of a probable difference between the thermal and optical ionization potentials. The factors must be addressed to ensure purposeful design and fabrication of devices operating on the principle of the interactions between these doped defects, the optical excitation, and the host semiconductor substrate. This review compiles the current body of knowledge on optically addressable defects in wide band gap semiconductors and known implementations into prototype devices. Viable pathways toward resolving these unknown design spaces toward future breakthroughs are identified.

title: A QUBO Formulation for Radio Base Station Deployment in Heterogeneous Networks
authors: Edson José Pacheco, Carlos Marcelo Pedroso
abstract: This paper proposes a Quadratic Unconstrained Binary Optimization (QUBO) formulation for the radio base station placement problem in heterogeneous 5G/6G networks. Deployment costs, coverage constraints, mutual exclusion, and Signal-to-Interference-plus-Noise Ratio (SINR) are encoded jointly into a single quadratic objective matrix. A SINR surrogate Hamiltonian captures the pairwise interference structure through signal-benefit terms on the diagonal and interference penalties on the off-diagonals,without linearization. The resulting two-phase decomposition separates discrete station selection (Phase I) from continuous position refinement (Phase II). Evaluation across scenarios with 50 to 500 demand points compares four pipelines: BIP+DE, QUBO+DE, QUBO+CD, and multi-scale QUBO refinement (QUBO-MS). All QUBO pipelines match the BIP cost-optimality ($3.80M) with 100% coverage. QUBO+DE achieves 12.34 dB network-wide SINR, exceeding the BIP+DE baseline by 2.71 dB due to SINR-aware Phase I selection. QUBO-MS progressively refines positions through cluster-constrained instances from50mto 0.5mresolution, reaching 10.77 dB and closing 62% of the gap relative to the continuous-space optimizer. The residual 2.11 dB gap stems from the pairwise cluster decomposition rather than grid quantization (<0.1 dB at 0.5 m). All multi-scale Phase II instances require fewer than 1,902 variables, ensuring compatibility with D-Wave Advantage quantum annealing hardware

title: A Fully Fault-Tolerant Multilevel Inverter for Uninterruptible Power Supply Applications
authors: Balram Kumar, Sankar Peddapati, VS Prasadarao K
abstract: For medical appliances and critical sectors, an uninterruptible power supply (UPS) is an indispensable electronic device that protects against power shortages for various loads. This article introduces a novel fault-tolerant multi-level inverter (MLI) designed to offer resilience against multiple switch faults and capable of operating in both symmetric and asymmetric voltage modes. Fault tolerance is achieved with the rated power by adding a redundant unit consisting of triacs and switches. The proposed converter is experimentally validated on a 500 W prototype, demonstrating robust performance under diverse fault conditions. In addition, a new parameter is proposed to assess the fault-tolerant capabilities of converter topologies, offering enhanced understanding of their reliability. Comparative analysis highlights the advantages of the proposed topology, showcasing its superior performance across various metrics. The fault-tolerant capability of the proposed converter presents a reliable solution for UPS systems in critical applications.

title: A Collaborative Neural Network With Adaptive Gradient Dynamics for Nonconvex Optimization
authors: Juliang Wang, Haoen Huang, Kun Deng, Zhigang Zeng
abstract: In this article, a collaborative neural network (CONN) with adaptive gradient dynamics is presented for addressing nonconvex optimization problems. Existing neural dynamics models often suffer from either prohibitive computational costs or unsatisfactory performance, which limits their practical effectiveness. To overcome this, an adaptive gradient neural dynamics (AGND) model is developed for local optimization in nonconvex problems, achieving reduced computational complexity and superior performance. Subsequently, a CONN is proposed that combines multiple AGND models for scattered searches with a metaheuristic rule that reinitializes neuronal states upon local convergence. Theoretical analysis establishes that the AGND model ensures local optimality, and further demonstrates the global convergence of the proposed CONN. Extensive experiments on numerous classical time-invariant nonconvex optimization benchmark problems illustrate the effectiveness and stability of the CONN. Moreover, the proposed method consistently achieves strong performance across diverse application problems, including both time-varying and time-invariant scenarios, such as robotic manipulator trajectory tracking, portfolio selection, and pressure vessel design.

title: Review for "Descriptor: LYNRED Mobility Dataset Multimodal Detection Subset (LYNRED-MDS)"
authors: N/A

title: optimize_anything: A Universal API for Optimizing any Text Parameter
authors: Lakshya A Agrawal, Donghyun Lee, Shangyin Tan, Wenjie Ma, Karim Elmaaroufi, Sanjit A. Seshia, Koushik Sen, Dan Klein, Ion Stoica, Joseph E. Gonzalez, Omar Khattab, Alexandros G. Dimakis, Matei Zaharia
publish: Proceedings of the ACM Conference on AI and Agentic Systems
abstract: None

title: Machine Learning Models for Behavioral Analysis in Criminal Investigation Training
authors: Kaushik Ghosh, Amit Kumar Goyal, Xudayberdiyev Baxtiyor, Numonova Mashkhura, Turabov Vaxid Turabovich, Utepbergenov Maxset Sadiqbaevich
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: Abstract— One of the vital aspects of criminal investigation training is behavioral analysis that influences the capacity of criminal investigators to perceive verbal and non-verbal indications, interview management and decision-making in the context of uncertainty. The old fashioned approaches to training are extensively relying on the subjective evaluation of the instructor which in most cases is inconsistent and unmeasurable. The given paper suggests an idea of a machine learning-based system of behavior analysis within the criminal investigation training, founded on multiproteic data including interview transcripts, characteristics of a speech, and communication logs. Sequential learning models are used to represent the temporal nature of the investigative action process and explainability aligned to rubrics is used to provide transparent and actionable feedback to trainees. Experimental results show that experimental multimodal approach can significantly enhance the accuracy of assessment, training and bias avoidance compared with the conventional and single modality methods. The results call for the use of machine learning in the context of supporting objective, ethical and adaptive training environments in the process of building future-ready criminal investigation training systems.

title: State of Health Estimation Lithium-ion Battery: Comparing Algorithms with Explainable Machine Learning
authors: Arun Jose, Sonam Shrivastava
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: Worldwide limits are being placed on polluting emissions while the development of more efficient, sustainable power sources continues to progress. Electric vehicles are considered major players in this transition, and will considerable accelerate the transformation even more obviously. In electric vehicles, the battery is often of lithium-ion type and features complex dynamics that make it challenging to estimate its parameters. This work presents a battery degradation investigation with model-based methodologies and explainable machine learning (xML) for state of health estimation. This research employs a battery data set from NASA, and MATLAB-based models have been constructed and trained with this data. A thorough examination of all the models and algorithms has been conducted, and a performance comparison is presented at the conclusion. Given that battery degradation is a complex, nonlinear, and noisy process, all the suggested methods are undoubtedly more effective in capturing these characteristics. The evaluation utilized error metrics such as MAE, MSE, and RMSE, and the study indicates that the random forest outperforms the other methods examined, demonstrating an MAE of 0.018. It also offers straightforward interpretability and effectively captures all discrete trends.

title: A Comparative Study of Electromagnetic-Field-Induced Hyperthermia Effects on Healthy Tissue and Breast Tumors of Different Sizes
authors: Ajay Kumar, Nitesh Kashyap, Ashish Raman, Vineet Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: Breast cancer is a significant health issue, and new diagnostic and treatment strategies are needed. It describes a design and prototype for a directed microwave breast tomography system that not only detects breast tumors but also uses localized hyperthermia therapy to enhance treatment (i.e., heat cancerous tissue while maintaining adjacent healthy tissue). The system uses a non-invasive, non-ionizing technique that is safer and more comfortable for patients than X-ray mammography and MRI. The system consists of a flexible antenna array, a microwave signal generator, and a control system to deliver the energy with precision. The system was evaluated for safety and efficacy. The design methodology and simulation results are outlined in this report. The paper provided a detailed dataset of reliable thermophysical property values for both healthy and cancerous breast tissues and thus standardized tissue property data, which is an ongoing effort to streamline and advance the research community\'s understanding of these important medical applications.

title: MIPS based 16 Bit Microprocessor Implementation
authors: Satish Kumar, Ankur Kumar, Naman Garg
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: Embedded applications prioritize faster execution of simple instructions at low power usage. Along with the above advantages, RISC based processors have low complexity and simple implementation and are therefore best suited for embedded and low power applications. FPGAs due to their reconfigurability are ideal for prototyping and low-cost testing of various processor architectures under constrained environment. Therefore, the paper aims to implement a RISC based processor which can process simple arithmetic, logical and other instructions. The circuit is designed using Verilog HDL and Xilinx Vivado. The designed circuit has been implemented on a FPGA board due the flexibility and reconfigurability provided by it.

title: Exploring AI Techniques for Enhancing Network Security
authors: Zwaib Ur Rehman, Mashood Ur Rehman Rana, Ali Haider, Muhammad Abdullah Qureshi, Meheryar Khan, Summaira Nosheen
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Machine Learning, and Intelligence (DataSciMI)
abstract: The rising use of Internet of Things (IoT) technologies and interconnected smart technologies has significantly increased the area of cyber-attack, making the network security an important topic in modern digital environments. Traditional intrusion detection systems (IDS) are usually limited by the ability to identify advanced and emerging threats at the same time protecting data privacy in decentralized environments. The study examines next-generation techniques of artificial intelligence (AI) to enhance the security of the network with a privacy-sensitive intrusion detection system, which combines deep learning (DL) and federated learning (FL). The suggested research methodology makes use of convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and long short-term memory (LSTM) to automatically derive spatial and temporal regularities in network traffic and identify malicious behavior with precision. The fact that federated learning enables decentralized model training in a variety of clients without sharing raw data promotes the confidentiality of data as well as adherence to regulations. The framework is tested on the CICIDS2017 dataset and exhaustive data preprocessing, feature engineering, and distributed training are involved in replicating the real-world smart network environments. Through the results of the experiments, it is indicated that there is a presence of great improvisations in the detection accuracy, precision, recall, as well as the F1-score in comparison to traditional centralized methods, without compromising the privacy, which is strong. These results imply that federated AI sequences can be viewed as a scalable, effective, and privacy-conscious way to secure smart network infrastructure and next-generation IoTs.

title: Securing Services Infrastructure: A Web-Based Automated Integrity Monitoring Framework for Linux Systems Using AIDE and Systemd Scheduling
authors: Muhammad Abdullah Khan, Fahad Bin Faiz, Arif Ur Rahman
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Machine Learning, and Intelligence (DataSciMI)
abstract: The security of critical service infrastructure has become a primary concern as cyber threats, configuration tampering, and unauthorized system modifications continue to escalate. This study presents a framework of file-integrity monitoring that is founded on the Advanced Intrusion Detection Environment of the OpenSUSE Linux servers. The system will have cryptographic integrity checking, secure baseline creation, automated scheduling through systemd timers, and email notifications to identify and report unauthorized modifications. A web-based administrative panel, developed using React and Django, provides real-time visibility into scan results, system health, configuration rules, and validation logs while enforcing strict access control for administrative operations. This solution strengthens infrastructure security by providing early detection of file modification attacks and configuration violations, offering a realistic basis for intrusion detection and secure configuration management.

title: Proactive Cloud Systems Reliability: A Multi-Model Approach to Node Failure Prediction
authors: Somit Jaiswal, Manjeet Singh, Amrit Raj, Lavkush Yadav, Kumar Shubhranshu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: Cloud services call for high reliability, motivating strategies to predict server node disruption before it arises. This paper proposes a multi-model failure prediction framework that combines deep learning for temporal analysis with ensemble learning for spatial analysis of machine states. Specifically, a Transformer-based sequence model captures time-series patterns in resource usage, replacing a conventional LSTM method, and an XGBoost classifier evaluates instantaneous device capabilities. The outputs are fused and passed into a second XGBoost model that ranks nodes by failure risk. To handle extremely uncommon failure events, the proposed model applies class-balancing techniques, including focal loss during training and SMOTE oversampling, and a cost-sensitive thresholding mechanism. The cost-sensitive approach allows tuning the false negative vs. false positive trade-off via weighting their "costs" to align with operational priorities for example, heavily penalizing missed failures on critical nodes. We evaluated our method on actual cloud trace data. Results show that it can achieve 80% recall, catching most imminent failures with about 100% precision at the chosen operating point, effectively providing early warning for most failures with zero false alerts. We also compared our method to a reference solution by Microsoft Research, which uses an LSTM and a Random Forest in a similar ensemble way. Moreover, explainable AI techniques, SHAP, is employed to interpret model outputs, identifying key signals that drive the predictions. These insights enhance the transparency and trustworthiness of the model.

title: Redefining Learning Efficacy with Fuzzy Logic Algorithm: Cognitive Load Analysis and Decision Making in Special Education Classroom
authors: Sugandha Sharma, Ritika Sanwal, Tojiboyev Jakhongir, Sultonova Nilufar, Jabbarov Ozimbay, Hikmatilla Abdunabiyev
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: The current work studies the application of fuzzy logic algorithms to enhance effectiveness of learning in special education classrooms using a combination of cognitive load analysis and adaptive decision-making. Traditional teaching methods, however, tend to struggle with the various learning needs of learning students with cognitive and developmental problems that cause added cognitive load and reduced involvement. This research proposes to define the fuzzy logic-based framework which describes the uncertainty and the variability in the learner's performance which leads to the individualization of the instructional strategies. The system utilizes student responses to determine cognitive load, student’s response to update make the system more appropriate in terms of difficulty and pacing of content activity. Experimental results have shown great levels of improvement in attention, understanding and task completion for students of different learning abilities. The study puts the potential of computational intelligence in aiding educators to make informed decisions in favor of inclusive and productive learning environments. Future work there can be done to integrate multimodal data and consider scale up to more diverse educational environments.

title: Design of a Smart Tourniquet Monitoring Device Based on Tiny Stretchable Conductive Fiber Sensing
authors: Wei-Cheng Chang, Jian-Feng Li, Ying-Nien Chou, Ming-Che Chen
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: This paper presents a Smart Tourniquet Monitoring Device based on a tiny Stretchable Conductive Fiber (SCF). The SCF is attached to a standard tourniquet and connected to a voltage divider circuit interfaced with the analog-to-digital converter of a system-on-chip. The device measures the voltage gradient variations generated during the stretching and releasing processes of the tourniquet. By analyzing these voltage characteristics, the system can effectively identify the operation behavior and usage state of the tourniquet, providing real-time and objective monitoring support for clinical hemostasis procedures.

title: Deep Learning Integrated Type 3 Fuzzy-PID Control for High-Performance IPMSM Drives in Electric Vehicles
authors: Vo Thanh Ha, Nguyen Duc Chien, Nguyen Gia Minh Thao
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: This paper introduces a deep-learning-assisted Type-3 fuzzy-Proportional Integral Derivative (PID) control method for Interior Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor (IPMSM) drives in smart electric vehicles. Addressing the limited adaptability of the conventional PID and Type-1 fuzzy controllers to nonlinear motor parameter variations and dynamic road conditions, this approach combines a Type-3 fuzzy logic system with a deep learning predictor based on a convolutional extended short-term memory network. The deeplearning module predicts torque and speed errors, enabling the Type-3 fuzzy inference system to adaptively tune PID gains by considering higher-order uncertainty. This hybrid structure improves the controller's robustness to parameter drift, noise, and load disturbances, requires minimal training data, and is compatible with standard vector-controlled IPMSM drive systems. Analytical comparisons demonstrate that the proposed controller has quicker convergence, reduced torque ripple, and a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$12 - 15\%$</tex> improvement in energy efficiency compared to conventional control schemes, offering a robust and energyefficient control solution for advanced electric drive systems.

title: PRESTO: Predictive and Real-Time Signal Control for Traffic Optimization via V2I Communication
authors: Kotaro Takahashi, Kouji Nishimura
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: The increasing speed and capacity of wireless communication have enabled the real-time, large-scale transmission and reception of data. This advancement has brought significant attention to Connected Vehicles, which are equipped with communication capabilities. Alongside connected vehicles, Vehicle-to-Everything (V2X) communication technology, which allows vehicles to exchange information with various entities, has also drawn considerable interest. While numerous V2X-driven use cases have been proposed to address various traffic problems, relatively few have focused specifically on alleviating traffic congestion. In response, this study proposes a novel traffic signal control method that utilizes Vehicle-to-Infrastructure (V2I) communication technology to mitigate congestion. The key feature of the proposed method is its ability to proactively acquire information about vehicles approaching an intersection and predict the direction each vehicle will take before the next signal phase change. By incorporating this prediction information into conventional traffic signal control logic, the method aims to improve overall traffic flow. The proposed method was evaluated using a traffic simulation that accurately reproduced actual traffic flow with a median relative error of 2%. The results of comparative experiments confirm that the proposed method significantly reduces the tendency towards bimodal distribution observed in conventional methods, leading to the formation of a more uniform and smooth traffic flow.

title: Flexible Shaping of the Sensing Zone of Battery-Free Wireless Systems Using an LCX Passive Repeater
authors: Jin Mitsugi, Osamu Tokumasu, Masashi Owaki, Taishi Oyanagi
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: This paper introduces a method to flexibly shape the sensing zone of 920 MHz band backscatter communication systems comprising battery-free wireless sensors. The shaping is realized by a passive repeater using a combination of a leaky coaxial cable (LCX) and an antenna. The passive repeater relays the wireless power and commands from an interrogator and delivers them to the backscatter sensors. The signals from backscatter sensors are relayed back to the interrogator through the passive repeater. This paper proposes a physical-distanceagnostic link model of a backscatter communication including an LCX passive repeater based on a set of experimental data. The advantage of using magnetic coupling antenna in the LCX passive repeater was also presented. A prototype of the passive repeater in 920 MHz was developed to reveal its practicality and performance by demonstrating non-line-of-sight wireless temperature sensing in an electro-magnetic shield room with less than 0.2% packet error rate.

title: Development of Business Diagram Creation Using AR Technology and Physical Blocks
authors: Kaoru Mitsuhashi, Iwao Sekita, Hiroshi Takeshita
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: The widespread use of smartphones and generative AI has led to a shortage of personnel capable of performing advanced PC operations, such as specialized diagramming, required in a DX (Digital Transformation) society. Despite this, there is a strong demand for creating business diagrams, such as organizational charts. This research proposes and develops a business diagramming tool utilizing Augmented Reality (AR) technology and physical blocks and investigates its effectiveness in terms of creation time and user experience. The tool's operation is designed to be more intuitive and easier than conventional PC-based methods. By arranging these physical blocks within the camera's view, users can create SWOT analysis diagrams (four-quadrant diagrams), organizational charts, and Gantt charts. The tool's effectiveness is subsequently validated through sample creation tasks while referencing existing diagrams. In this result, AR-based operation enables faster diagram creation compared to PC-based methods, and the tool is more intuitive and easier to understand.

title: Enhancing Human Faces Generated with Deep Learning
authors: Ramez Ghabrial
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Computer Theory and Applications (ICCTA)
abstract: The use of Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) to produce realistic images, particularly human faces, has gained popularity. Although it can produce good results, one popular version, the Deep Convolutional GAN (DCGAN), frequently produces unnatural or unclear faces. In this study, the DCGAN model was enhanced in a number of ways to produce faces that are more distinct and intricate. Using residual connections, deepening the network, and altering the model's training process are some of these enhancements. Following generation, a face restoration model known as Generative Facial Prior (GFP-GAN) was employed to further improve the results. GFP-GAN uses a pretrained model called StyleGAN2 to help restore and sharpen facial features while keeping the person's identity. Tests were conducted on the smaller selected Face-Lite dataset. Results showed that the improved model produced clearer, more stable and better-looking face images. This project shows how upgrading the DCGAN structure and training process can significantly improve the quality of generated faces.

title: Context-Aware Gaze-Based Interaction for Users with Motor Disabilities: A Low-Cost and Calibration-Free Approach
authors: Habiba Mekay, Wessam Mohamed Reda, Rawan Wagih, Doaa Shawky
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Computer Theory and Applications (ICCTA)
abstract: Conventional user interfaces that rely on mouse or touch input prove difficult to use for those with motor disabilities. While gaze-based interaction offers a promising handsfree alternative, existing solutions frequently require expensive hardware, precise calibration, or fail to adapt effectively to dynamic and cluttered web environments. This paper proposes a low-cost, calibration-free, and context-aware gaze interaction system designed to operate entirely within the browser using standard webcams. The proposed approach uses MediaPipe's FaceMesh to extract facial landmarks and introduces a contextaware scoring mechanism that dynamically identifies actionable DOM elements based on gaze proximity, visibility, and semantic type. This differs greatly from prior work that maps gaze directly to screen coordinates as it allows for more accurate navigation. To enhance interaction reliability, we integrate temporal filtering strategies such as sliding window smoothing and dwell-time detection, which reduce noise and make sure of user intent. The system was tested using various enhancements and evaluated in real-time across multiple browser-based scenarios, including dynamic and dense interfaces. Preliminary results indicate a mean gaze estimation error of 10% pixels, with 80% accuracy in selecting the intended DOM element under cluttered layouts. The addition of contextual scoring and temporal smoothing improved precision compared to naive gaze-to-element mapping. This paper shows the feasibility of delivering calibration-free, accessible, real-time gaze interaction through standard web technologies with no specialized hardware. The proposed system holds great potential for improving digital accessibility for users with motor impairments.

title: Free Space Optical Channel Under Fog: Enhanced Performance Using MDRZ Technique
authors: Moustafa S. Ahmed, Amira I. Zaki, Moustafa H. Aly, Heba A. Fayed
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Computer Theory and Applications (ICCTA)
abstract: Data transmission by free space optics becomes an exemplary solution for high-speed communication in the last mile, providing benefits such as high bandwidth and low bit error rates. However, its performance is affected by climate conditions, including rain, fog, and other factors. This study focuses on the design enhancement of the transmitter side to improve the channel quality, achieving longer propagation range with high received power, higher rate, lower rate of bit error in short distances and low implementation costs. Our research showed the importance of selecting an appropriate modulation scheme to enhance the link quality over longer distance. So, Modified Duobinary Return to Zero (MDRZ) technique successfully extended the link distance to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 3 0 0 ~ m}$</tex> with the best quality factor and lower Bit Error Rate (BER) under light fog at an operating wavelength of 1550 nm. Through simulations conducted via OptiSystem software, utilizing three modulation layouts, NRZ, RZ, and MDRZ, the system achieves the best received optical power (ROP) and evaluation of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$Q$</tex>-factor when applying MDRZ and RZ modulation layouts while obtaining better and lower BER when utilizing the original NRZ modulation layout. The outcomes confirm that the enhanced MDRZ modulation improves the transmission quality and extends the communication distance under foggy climate.

title: FICS: Foreground-Informed Concept Segmentation for Robust, Object-Centric Explainability
authors: Muhammad Imran Khalid, Asma Komal, Faiza Ayyob, Danish Atique
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Machine Learning, and Intelligence (DataSciMI)
abstract: Trust in deep neural networks is vital for high-stakes applications. Current XAI methods have flaws: attribution creates noisy heatmaps, while concept-based methods like CRAFT suffer from background leakage and high costs. We introduce Foreground-Informed Concept Segmentation (FICS), which redefines concept discovery as saliency-driven clustering focused on the object. FICS first performs foreground isolation via segmentation, then applies saliency-based K-Means to identify semantically similar parts like wheels or ears. Extensive experiments on COCO and ImageNet show FICS achieves state-of-the-art fidelity, outperforming CRAFT with lower Deletion AOC (0.7512 vs 0.8491) and higher Insertion AOC (0.8915 vs 0.7713). Ablations confirm the importance of saliency guidance for human-aligned interpretability. Qualitatively, FICS produces sharp, noise-free, part-based concepts within object boundaries only. By combining semantic accuracy and efficiency, FICS enhances AI explainability toward reliable, human-consistent visual clarifications. Additionally, it offers a trade-off between object-centricity and fidelity.

title: Artificially Structured Surfaces for Controlled Beam Bifurcation and Radiation Amplification in 5G Antennas
authors: Harish Raizada, Nitesh Kashyap
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: In order to increase gain and split the beam, this research proposes a Metasurface for the fifth-generation Microstrip patch antenna operating at 2.4GHz. Extremely 5G mobile networks require directional multi-beam antennas, which array antennas can provide. However, array antennas operating at lower frequencies increase the network's overall complexity and scalability. As a result, the suggested metasurface exhibits multi-beam and high-gain characteristics while guiding surface currents. Circular Split Ring Resonator (CSRR) or Circular-Shaped unit cell designs are both used to implement a metasurface composed of a single circular patch antenna. The beam can be divided by introducing counter currents within the unit cell through manipulation of this hybrid metasurface arrangement. The FR-4 Substrate was used to create the hybrid metasurface and antenna. According to the data, the antenna has a reflection coefficient of less than -10 dB and exhibits good resonance at 2.4 GHz. Because unit cells are arranged on the metasurface, Future 5G Pico mobile base stations in high-power, interference-prone urban or suburban locations would benefit greatly from this antenna.

title: Kokborok Numbers System Identification Using Deep Learning Techniques
authors: Mina Nath, Suman Debbarma, Robin Debbarma, Rahul Sutradhar, Jayanta Pal, Swanirbhar Majumder
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: Kokborok is an under-resourced language spoken by the Borok community of Tripura and neighboring regions. One of its distinctive linguistic components is its native number system, which differs significantly from widely used decimal-based systems. This paper presents a deep learning-based approach for identifying spoken Kokborok numerals (1-10) using spectrogram representations of speech signals. Audio samples collected from native speakers were pre-processed and converted into spectrogram images, which were then used to train a convolutional neural network based on the SqueezeNet architecture. Experimental results demonstrate the feasibility of applying lightweight deep learning models to minority language speech recognition, while also highlighting the challenges of data scarcity and model overfitting. The study contributes to digital preservation and technological inclusion of the Kokborok language.

title: Trace-Level Analysis of Information Contamination in Multi-Agent Systems
authors: Anna Mazhar, Huzaifa Suri, Sainyam Galhotra
publish: Proceedings of the ACM Conference on AI and Agentic Systems
abstract: None

title: Simulation Approaches for Supporting Microservice Architectures: A Systematic Review
authors: Andila Asim, Nour Ali
publish: ACM Transactions on Software Engineering and Methodology
abstract: None

title: 2026 International Conference on Current Research in Artificial Intelligence and Data Science (ICCRAIDS)
